features.behavior: The KIE repository publishes an installable OpenAI-compatible transport package that starts a loopback-only server, requires bearer authentication, exposes readiness without secrets, loads KIE credentials only from the server environment, rejects client-supplied credentials and remote output URLs, and normalizes errors into OpenAI-shaped JSON.

verification.expected: Package tests pass for loopback binding, bearer auth, readiness, secret rejection, URL validation, and normalized errors.

features.context_note: Implement inside the KIE monorepo and reuse @felores/kie-ai-core. Security boundary: the browser must never supply or inherit KIE_API_KEY.
features.evidence: KIE commit c9f034a on feat/kie-openai-server-contract; package tests 13/13 passed; core and package typechecks passed; npm publish --dry-run passed; correctness, security, and package follow-up reviews found no remaining actionable issues.

features.behavior: The KIE transport exposes synchronous POST /v1/images/generations and POST /v1/images/edits routes backed by the allowlisted Nano Banana and GPT Image 2 adapters, with exact ratio, resolution, count, MIME, and reference limits; server-side reference upload; deterministic mask and unsupported-field rejection before paid work; idempotent request correlation; polling and timeout handling; and OpenAI image data objects containing downloaded base64 results.

verification.expected: Image contract and journal tests pass for generation, reference edits, mask and unsupported-setting rejection, one upload per source, idempotent submission, polling, timeout, and OpenAI-shaped base64 responses.

features.context_note: Follow the plan's Initial Model Matrix. KIE edits use uploaded image references only; reject multipart masks before journal reservation because neither selected KIE adapter honors OpenAI mask semantics.
features.evidence: Uncommitted KIE worktree on feat/kie-openai-server-contract; OpenAI transport tests 31/31 passed, core tests 56/56 passed, both package typechecks and git diff --check passed; Infini native OpenAI multipart mask regression test 6/6 passed; final security review found no remaining blockers.

features.behavior: The KIE transport exposes OpenAI-shaped video task creation and retrieval routes for Seedance 2, validates provider-specific fields before submission, uploads local image, video, and audio references before paid work begins, preserves multimodal references and native-audio controls, normalizes task states and terminal errors, and supports authenticated callback reconciliation without creating a second task.

verification.expected: Video task, provider validation, upload, polling, callback authentication, restart reconciliation, and no-duplicate-submission tests pass.

features.context_note: Keep the create/read API asynchronous and distinguish local task ids from KIE provider ids. Callback auth and idempotent reconciliation are load-bearing.
features.evidence: Uncommitted KIE worktree on feat/kie-openai-server-contract; OpenAI transport tests 48/48 passed (17 video + 31 image), core typecheck passed, package typecheck passed, git diff --check passed. Video routes cover creation, status polling, content streaming, callback reconciliation with token auth, multimodal reference uploads (image/video/audio), native-audio controls, frame control, restart recovery, and provider failure mapping.

features.behavior: The KIE repository exports the transport package, includes it in root build, test, typecheck, and bundle workflows, provides install and runtime documentation for loopback and container use, publishes a tested exact package version, and includes generated OpenAI transport documentation in the existing documentation workflow.

verification.command: npm --prefix ../mcp/kie-ai-mcp-server test && npm --prefix ../mcp/kie-ai-mcp-server run typecheck && npm --prefix ../mcp/kie-ai-mcp-server run build
verification.expected: The KIE monorepo test, typecheck, and build workflows pass with the transport package included.

features.context_note: Publish from the KIE monorepo only after U1-U3 contracts pass. Infini must consume the exact published version, never a workspace or git dependency.
features.evidence: Uncommitted KIE worktree on feat/kie-openai-server-contract; root test 104/104 passed (core 56 + openai 48), root typecheck 4/4 packages passed, root build 4/4 packages passed, npm pack dry-run 21 files self-contained with no source/tests/private deps. Fixed pre-existing MCP and CLI typecheck scripts (tsc -b --noEmit incompatible with composite project references; changed to tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit).

features.behavior: Canvas Agent loads the exact published KIE transport package, mounts it behind existing loopback and bearer protection, exposes readiness through its established health/config surfaces without secrets, starts successfully without KIE provider credentials, and allows existing native image generation, alpha-mask Local Edit, and Seedance workflows to use the local KIE channel without exposing provider credentials to browser persistence, frontend state, logs, child agents, or exported bundles.

verification.command: bun run --cwd canvas-agent build && bun run --cwd web typecheck
verification.expected: Canvas Agent builds with the pinned transport and the web typecheck passes with existing native request shapes unchanged.

features.context_note: Mount behind the existing Canvas Agent boundary; do not change Infini's native image.ts or video.ts contracts. KIE credentials remain server-only and optional at startup.

features.behavior: Infini has automated and manual release evidence proving GPT Image 2 text generation, native OpenAI alpha-mask editing, KIE-backed masked editing behavior, Seedance 2 text and multimodal generation, task resume after restart, browser-local asset persistence, unsupported-option rejection before paid submission, abort and timeout behavior, and absence of KIE secrets from browser persistence, bundle output, logs, child-agent environments, and unrelated repositories.

verification.command: bun run --cwd web test:run && bun run --cwd canvas-agent test
verification.expected: Focused web and Canvas Agent contract suites pass and cover every provider path and security invariant named in the plan.

features.context_note: Use focused contract tests plus the plan's manual paid-generation checklist. Keep the changelog summary high-level and move implemented todo items to pending-test only after behavior works.
